title: knowledge/ scaffolding: directory, requirements.txt, pom.xml, curated_baseline.md stub
content:
Create the `knowledge/` directory and its static files. Nothing in this issue runs code — it just establishes the skeleton that the other issues build on.

## Files to create

### `knowledge/requirements.txt`
Pin all Python dependencies needed by `build.py`:
- `sentence-transformers` (for bge-small-en-v1.5 embeddings)
- `rank-bm25` (for BM25 index)
- `python-frontmatter` (for parsing YAML frontmatter in markdown)
- Any transitive pinning needed for reproducibility (use `pip-compile` or equivalent)

### `knowledge/pom.xml`
Maven coordinates:
```xml
<groupId>com.manetu</groupId>
<artifactId>mpe-knowledge</artifactId>
<version>${project.version}</version>
<packaging>jar</packaging>
```
Version is injected at build time via `-Dproject.version=...`. Include `distributionManagement` pointing to GitHub Packages (`https://maven.pkg.github.com/manetu/policyengine`).

### `knowledge/curated_baseline.md`
Initial hand-curated MPE essentials stub (~1–2 K tokens). Plain UTF-8 Markdown, no frontmatter, safe to prepend verbatim to an LLM system prompt. Cover at minimum:
- What PolicyEngine is and what problem it solves
- Core PORC model (Principals, Operations, Resources, Conditions)
- PolicyDomain YAML structure overview
- Key CLI commands (`mpe lint`, `mpe test`, `mpe serve`)

This file will evolve — the initial version just needs to be a useful skeleton that a developer can refine.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `knowledge/requirements.txt` exists with pinned versions for all needed deps
- [ ] `knowledge/pom.xml` is valid Maven POM with correct coordinates; version is a placeholder/property, not hardcoded
- [ ] `knowledge/curated_baseline.md` contains meaningful content (~500–2000 tokens), no frontmatter, renders as valid Markdown
- [ ] No code files yet (build.py, Makefile) — those are in subsequent issues

title: build.py: document chunker — walk docs/docs/ and split at H2 + code-fence boundaries
content:
Implement the document chunking phase of `knowledge/build.py`. This is the first pipeline stage; its output feeds the embedding and BM25 stages.

## Behavior

Walk all `.md` files under `docs/docs/` (recursively). For each file:

1. Parse YAML frontmatter (if present) via `python-frontmatter`.
2. Split the body at **H2 (`##`) heading boundaries**. Each H2 section becomes one or more chunks.
3. Within an H2 section, if the section exceeds a configurable token budget (default: ~500 tokens estimated by word count), further split at **code-fence boundaries** (``` ``` ```). Prefer not to break mid-fence.
4. For each chunk, emit:
   ```json
   {
     "id": "<stable-hash>",
     "path": "docs/docs/concepts/pbac.md",
     "heading_path": ["PBAC", "How It Works"],
     "frontmatter": { ... },
     "text": "..."
   }
   ```
   - `id`: SHA-256 of `path + heading_path + text` (first 16 hex chars is fine), stable for the same doc commit
   - `heading_path`: ordered array of headings from H1 down to the current H2 (include H1 title if present as first element)
   - `text`: the raw Markdown text of the chunk, including the heading line itself

## Output
Write all chunks (one JSON object per line) to `knowledge/target/chunks.jsonl`. Log chunk count to stdout.

## Module structure
`build.py` should be organized so the chunker is a clearly delimited function/module that can be called independently and tested. The top-level `main()` calls stages in order.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `knowledge/build.py` exists with a `chunk_documents(docs_root) -> list[dict]` function
- [ ] Running `python knowledge/build.py --stage=chunk` against the real `docs/docs/` tree produces a valid `knowledge/target/chunks.jsonl`
- [ ] Every line in `chunks.jsonl` is valid JSON with all required fields (`id`, `path`, `heading_path`, `frontmatter`, `text`)
- [ ] The same docs commit produces identical `chunks.jsonl` output on repeated runs (deterministic)
- [ ] Chunk count logged to stdout

title: build.py: embedding pipeline — generate vectors.bin using bge-small-en-v1.5
content:
Implement the embedding stage of `knowledge/build.py`. Reads chunks produced by the chunker and emits a packed float32 binary.

## Behavior

1. Load the `BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5` model via `sentence-transformers` (downloads and caches to `~/.cache/huggingface/` on first run).
2. Encode `chunk["text"]` for each chunk in `chunks.jsonl` order, producing a 384-dimensional float32 vector per chunk.
3. Write all vectors row-major to `knowledge/target/vectors.bin` as raw `float32` (little-endian). Row `i` corresponds to chunk `i` in `chunks.jsonl` order. Total bytes = `chunk_count × 384 × 4`.

## Performance
- Use batched encoding (batch size 32 or configurable) for CPU efficiency.
- Log estimated time and throughput (chunks/sec) to stdout.

## Determinism
For the same input `chunks.jsonl` and same model version, the output `vectors.bin` must be byte-identical across runs (no random seeds, no float non-determinism from parallelism). Pin model revision if needed.

## `manifest.json` fields contributed by this stage
```json
{
  "embed_model": "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5",
  "embed_dims": 384
}
```

## Module structure
`embed_chunks(chunks: list[dict]) -> np.ndarray` function in `build.py`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `embed_chunks()` function exists in `build.py`
- [ ] Running `python knowledge/build.py --stage=embed` produces `knowledge/target/vectors.bin`
- [ ] File size = `chunk_count × 384 × 4` bytes (validated in script with assertion)
- [ ] Row `i` is the float32 embedding of chunk `i` from `chunks.jsonl`
- [ ] Two runs on same input produce byte-identical `vectors.bin`
- [ ] `embed_model` and `embed_dims` values are accessible for `manifest.json` assembly

title: build.py: BM25 lexical index — build, serialize, and document binary format
content:
Implement the BM25 index stage of `knowledge/build.py` and document the binary serialization format in the spec's Consumer Contract section.

## Behavior

1. Build a BM25 index over all chunk texts using `rank-bm25` (`BM25Okapi` with `k1=1.2, b=0.75`).
2. Serialize the index to `knowledge/target/lexical-index.bin` in a documented binary format (see below).
3. After implementation, update `specs/s-1ka5_knowledge_artifact.md` to fill in the "BM25 serialization format" placeholder with the actual binary layout.

## Binary serialization format (to implement + document)

Design a simple, language-agnostic binary format. Suggested layout:

```
Header (16 bytes):
  magic:    4 bytes  "BM25"
  version:  2 bytes  uint16 LE, value=1
  k1:       4 bytes  float32 LE
  b:        4 bytes  float32 LE
  reserved: 2 bytes  zeros

Vocabulary section:
  vocab_size: 4 bytes uint32 LE
  For each term (vocab_size entries):
    term_len:  2 bytes uint16 LE
    term:      term_len bytes UTF-8
    idf:       4 bytes float32 LE

Postings section:
  For each term (same order as vocabulary):
    posting_count: 4 bytes uint32 LE
    For each posting:
      chunk_id:  4 bytes uint32 LE  (index into chunks.jsonl)
      tf_score:  4 bytes float32 LE
```

The exact format is flexible — document whatever you implement. The key constraint is that a Clojure consumer can deserialize it without calling Python.

## Spec update
After implementing, update `specs/s-1ka5_knowledge_artifact.md` section "BM25 serialization format" with the actual binary layout (byte offsets, types, endianness).

## Module structure
`build_lexical_index(chunks: list[dict]) -> bytes` function in `build.py`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `build_lexical_index()` exists in `build.py` and writes `knowledge/target/lexical-index.bin`
- [ ] Binary file starts with magic bytes `BM25` (for basic validation by consumers)
- [ ] `specs/s-1ka5_knowledge_artifact.md` "BM25 serialization format" section filled in with actual byte layout
- [ ] A small round-trip test: serialize → deserialize in Python, confirm a known term's IDF and posting list are recoverable
- [ ] Two runs on same input produce byte-identical `lexical-index.bin`

title: build.py: artifact assembly — pack JAR, emit manifest.json, log sha256 fingerprint
content:
Implement the final assembly stage of `knowledge/build.py` that combines all intermediate outputs into the distributable JAR.

## Behavior

1. Read `chunks.jsonl`, `vectors.bin`, `lexical-index.bin` from `knowledge/target/`.
2. Read `curated_baseline.md` from `knowledge/`.
3. Build `manifest.json`:
   ```json
   {
     "manifest_version": 1,
     "mpe_version": "<from git describe>",
     "embed_model": "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5",
     "embed_dims": 384,
     "chunk_count": <line count of chunks.jsonl>,
     "build_timestamp": "<ISO-8601 UTC>",
     "doc_commit_sha": "<git rev-parse HEAD>"
   }
   ```
4. Validate consistency: assert `chunk_count` == line count of `chunks.jsonl` == row count implied by `vectors.bin` size.
5. Assemble a ZIP (`.jar`) at `knowledge/target/mpe-knowledge-<version>.jar`:
   ```
   manifest.json
   curated_baseline.md
   chunks.jsonl
   vectors.bin
   lexical-index.bin
   META-INF/maven/com.manetu/mpe-knowledge/pom.xml
   ```
6. Log a sha256 fingerprint of the jar contents to stdout (excluding `META-INF/MANIFEST.MF` timestamp bytes if Java adds one). Format: `sha256: <hex>  mpe-knowledge-<version>.jar`

## Version sourcing
Use `git describe --tags --always --dirty`. If not in a git repo, fallback to `dev`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Running `python knowledge/build.py` (no flags) runs all stages end-to-end and produces `knowledge/target/mpe-knowledge-<version>.jar`
- [ ] JAR contains exactly: `manifest.json`, `curated_baseline.md`, `chunks.jsonl`, `vectors.bin`, `lexical-index.bin`, `META-INF/maven/com.manetu/mpe-knowledge/pom.xml`
- [ ] `manifest.json` parses and all required fields are present with correct types
- [ ] `manifest_version` is `1`
- [ ] `chunk_count` matches `chunks.jsonl` line count
- [ ] `embed_dims × chunk_count × 4` == `vectors.bin` file size (validated by script)
- [ ] sha256 fingerprint logged to stdout on every run
- [ ] Two runs on the same git commit (same docs state) produce matching sha256 fingerprints (modulo `build_timestamp`)

title: Makefile: knowledge-build, knowledge-install, knowledge-deploy targets
content:
Add `knowledge/Makefile` and wire its targets into the root `Makefile`.

## `knowledge/Makefile`

```makefile
VENV := knowledge/.venv
PYTHON := $(VENV)/bin/python
PIP := $(VENV)/bin/pip
VERSION ?= $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo "dev")
TARGET := knowledge/target/mpe-knowledge-$(VERSION).jar

.PHONY: knowledge-build knowledge-install knowledge-deploy knowledge-clean

$(VENV): knowledge/requirements.txt
	python3 -m venv $(VENV)
	$(PIP) install --upgrade pip
	$(PIP) install -r knowledge/requirements.txt

knowledge-build: $(VENV)  ## Build the knowledge JAR
	mkdir -p knowledge/target
	$(PYTHON) knowledge/build.py

knowledge-install: knowledge-build  ## Install JAR to ~/.m2 (for local iamlite dev)
	mvn install:install-file \
	  -Dfile=$(TARGET) \
	  -DgroupId=com.manetu \
	  -DartifactId=mpe-knowledge \
	  -Dversion=$(VERSION) \
	  -Dpackaging=jar

knowledge-deploy: knowledge-build  ## Deploy JAR to GitHub Packages (CI only)
	mvn deploy:deploy-file \
	  -Dfile=$(TARGET) \
	  -DgroupId=com.manetu \
	  -DartifactId=mpe-knowledge \
	  -Dversion=$(VERSION) \
	  -Dpackaging=jar \
	  -DrepositoryId=github \
	  -Durl=https://maven.pkg.github.com/manetu/policyengine

knowledge-clean:
	rm -rf knowledge/target
```

## Root `Makefile` additions
Add the following targets (delegating to `knowledge/Makefile`):
```makefile
knowledge-build: ## Build the knowledge artifact JAR
	@$(MAKE) -f knowledge/Makefile knowledge-build

knowledge-install: ## Install knowledge JAR to ~/.m2
	@$(MAKE) -f knowledge/Makefile knowledge-install

knowledge-deploy: ## Deploy knowledge JAR to GitHub Packages (CI)
	@$(MAKE) -f knowledge/Makefile knowledge-deploy
```

Also extend the `clean` target to call `knowledge-clean`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `make knowledge-build` from repo root creates `knowledge/target/mpe-knowledge-<version>.jar`
- [ ] On first run, a Python venv is created under `knowledge/.venv` and deps installed from `requirements.txt`
- [ ] On subsequent runs, venv creation is skipped (Make dependency on `requirements.txt`)
- [ ] `make knowledge-install` runs `mvn install:install-file` and the jar is findable at `~/.m2/repository/com/manetu/mpe-knowledge/`
- [ ] `make knowledge-deploy` runs `mvn deploy:deploy-file` targeting GitHub Packages (does not require auth locally — auth is provided by CI environment)
- [ ] `make clean` removes `knowledge/target/`
- [ ] `make help` lists the three new targets with descriptions

title: GH Actions: publish-knowledge.yml — build and publish knowledge artifact on release
content:
Add `.github/workflows/publish-knowledge.yml` that triggers on release tags, builds the knowledge artifact, publishes to GitHub Packages, and attaches it to the GitHub Release.

## Workflow design

Trigger: `on: release: types: [created]` (same pattern as `release-go.yml`).

Permissions:
```yaml
permissions:
  contents: write      # attach release asset
  packages: write      # push to GitHub Packages
```

### Steps

1. `actions/checkout@v4` with `fetch-depth: 0` (needed for `git describe`)
2. `actions/setup-python@v5` with Python 3.11; cache `pip` keyed on `knowledge/requirements.txt`
3. `make knowledge-build` — produces `knowledge/target/mpe-knowledge-<VERSION>.jar`
4. `actions/setup-java@v4` with Java 17 (Temurin); configure `settings.xml` for GitHub Packages auth using `${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}`
5. `make knowledge-deploy` — `mvn deploy` to `https://maven.pkg.github.com/manetu/policyengine`
6. `softprops/action-gh-release@v2` — attach the jar as a release asset

### Maven settings.xml
The workflow must write a `~/.m2/settings.xml` that injects GitHub Packages credentials:
```xml
<server>
  <id>github</id>
  <username>${env.GITHUB_ACTOR}</username>
  <password>${env.GITHUB_TOKEN}</password>
</server>
```

### Version passing
Extract version from the release tag:
```bash
VERSION=${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}
```
Pass as `VERSION=$VERSION make knowledge-build` so the jar filename is deterministic.

### Cache
Cache the Hugging Face model cache (`~/.cache/huggingface/`) keyed on `knowledge/requirements.txt` hash to avoid re-downloading the ~33 MB model on every release.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Workflow file exists at `.github/workflows/publish-knowledge.yml`
- [ ] Triggers only on `release: types: [created]` (not on push/PR)
- [ ] Uses pinned action SHAs (matching the pattern in other workflows in this repo)
- [ ] `knowledge-build` step produces the JAR with version matching the release tag
- [ ] `knowledge-deploy` step pushes to GitHub Packages without manual token configuration
- [ ] JAR is attached as a release asset on the GitHub Release page
- [ ] Hugging Face model is cached between runs
- [ ] Workflow can be re-run without failure if JAR already exists in release assets (idempotent)
